rulings.Case Title: PATRICK KELLY VS. THOMAS WHITE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 19, 2012, line 25. DEFENDANT THOMAS WHITE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff's claims are adequately pled, are not uncertain and are not necessarily irreconcilable with the allegations in the complaint. The fact that plaintiff may not obtain tort damages for his fourth claims does not render that claim deficient. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK KELLY VS. THOMAS WHITE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton And Motion Dismissing Or Staying Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 19, 2012, line 25. DEFENDANT THOMAS WHITE'S NOTICE OF MOITON AND MOTION DISMISSING OR STAYING ACTION. Denied. Assuming that Mexico and Thailand are suitable alternative fora, Defendant fails to show that the public and private factors weigh in favor of granting this motion. Plaintiff's declaration, which complies with Code of Civil Procedure 2015.5, states that plaintiff is a California resident who is only temporarily residing in Thailand. Plaintiff's choice of forum is entitled to great weight. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM F MOORE VS. MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA X, A CALIFRONIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 19, 2012, line 5. DEFENDANTS EPISCOPAL COMMUNITY SERVICES OF SAN FRANCISCO AND CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Viewing the facts most favorably to plaintiff, defendant had a duty of ordinary care not to engage in any conduct that posed a safety risk to the resident s of the Rose Hotel including plaintiff. There is a triable issue whether defendant breached its duty of ordinary care by advocating for Mr. Pious to remain on the premises despite his documented history of violence. Judge Kahn discloses that he is friends with Mr. Werth, one of the counsel for defendant, coached Judge Kahn's son in little league baseball last year, yet Judge Kahn believes that he can be fair and impartial to both sides. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAZMIN BARRERA et al VS. HARRY E. JENSEN III et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 19, 2012, line 6. DEFENDANT HARRY JENSEN AND TOD SCHLESINGER'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied without prejudice to filing a motion in limine seeking to exclude evidence relating to the allegations sought to be stricken. With trial less than two weeks away, no useful purpose is served by striking any allegations. The parties need to focus on what evidence is admissible at the trial, which is properly the function of the trial judge, not a law and motion judge. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CENTURY 21 PINNACLE AKA PINNACLE HOMES & ESTATES VS. JOSE L. CAMPOS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion/Pettiiton To Compel Arb And Request To Stay Procedings Until Completion Of Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 19, 2012, line 7. PLAINTIFF CENTURY 21 PINNACLE AKA PINNACLE HOMES & ESTATES' Notice Of Hearing On Motion/Petition To Compel Arbitration And Request To Stay Procedings Until Completion Of Arbitration. Granted. The filing of this action was not a waiver or forfeiture of the right to arbitrate. Paragraph 19B(2)(iv) permits the filing of an action to record a lis pendens. The failure to request mediation earlier is not a waiver or forfeiture of the right to arbitrate. Paragraph 19A provides that the waiver of attorney's fees is the penalty for failing to pursue mediation. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO MONTEAGUDO VS. 267 GREEN ST., LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Eduardo Monteagudo Motion To Compel Defendant Responses To Discovery; To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 19, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 3. Plaintiff Eduardo Monteagudo Motion To Compel Defendant Responses To Discovery; To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to Compel GRANTED in part. Order to issue at hearing. Parties to meet and confer at hearing on date for service of Defendant's amended verification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROSS MIRKARIMI VS. CITY AND C0UNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Disqualify Sf City Attorney From Representing Mayor Edwin M Lee; Memo Of P & A And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 19, 2012, line 24. PETITIONER ROSS MIRKARIMI'S Notice Of Motion To Disqualify SF City Attorney From Representing Mayor Edwin M Lee. Denied. Because the City Attorney is representing the Mayor in his official capacity with respect to actions he took on behalf of the City in accordance with a provision of the Charter authorizing the Mayor to take such actions, the City Attorney is not barred from representing the Mayor in this case. The retention of outside counsel by the Ethics Commission and the Board of Supervisors moots Petitioner's argument that there is a conflict between the City Attorney's representations of the Mayor in this case and the City Attorney's representation of the Ethics Commission and the Board of Supervisors with respect to the official misconduct charges against Petitioner. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASOCIATION, INC. DBA GREAT VS. SAN MATEO CONVALESCENT HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA CORP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment; Application For Right To Attach Order; Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment After Hearing And Pos (San Mateo Convalescent Hospital A California Corporation)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 19, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1. PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASOCIATION, INC. Application For Right To Attach Order (San Mateo Convalescent Hospital A California Corporation). Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Application for Right to Attach Order GRANTED. Order for Issuance of Writ of Attachment to issue at hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MYRA DANIELS VS. ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 19, 2012, line 19. DEFENDANT ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled in its entirety. At this stage of the case, the complaint is not barred by the statute of limitations or precluded by the prior settlements. The court cannot determine that as a matter of law that the settlement agreements bar claims arising from back-ups occurring after July 9, 2008. The statute of limitations does not necessarily and clearly bar every claim The first, third, and fifth causes of action are not uncertain. The ninth cause of action is not uncertain or barred by the statute of limitations and plaintiff has standing to pursue that claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MYRA DANIELS VS. ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Strike; Req For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 19, 2012, line 19. DEFENDANT ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP'S MOTION To Strike Complaint. Granted as to the punitive damages allegations with ten days leave to amend and denied as to the third cause of action. The punitive damages allegations are insufficient, but plaintiff is granted leave, if she can do sol in good faith, to allege ultimate facts showing that the defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. A motion to strike is not the proper procedure to challenge the entirety of a claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
